style: Pale garnet, high acid, high tannin, red cherry, rose, dried herbs, tar, earthy notes. Complex and ageworthy.

conclusions: Long ageing potential (10-30+ years); normally drink after 3-5 years minimum

common_student_errors: Calling it Pinot Noir due to pale colour
Underestimating tannin because of pale colour
Missing the complexity under the austerity
Confusing rose and floral with sweetness
Treating tar and leather as faults

misconceptions_addressed.correction: Nebbiolo demonstrates that high tannin is completely independent of colour; pale colour can hide serious structure

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Never infer tannin from colour alone
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Palate assessment of tannin is essential; colour is unreliable for predicting structure

misconceptions_addressed.misconception: Tar and earthy notes are faults
misconceptions_addressed.correction: Tar, leather, and forest floor are positive tertiary characteristics in Nebbiolo

coaching_hints: Never infer tannin from colour alone.
Feel the tannin texture on your palate — is it harsh or structured?
Notice the pale colour despite full tannin — a key diagnostic feature.
Can you identify the rose and herbal notes under the structure?

causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Pale colour despite high tannin extraction
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Pale garnet with serious astringent mouthfeel

causal_reasoning_paths.cause: Cool Piedmont climate and high acidity
causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Red cherry and sour cherry rather than black fruit
causal_reasoning_paths.example_in_wine: Bright red fruit despite long ageing capacity

causal_reasoning_paths.cause: Long bottle age and reductive evolution
causal_reasoning_paths.effect: Tertiary development of tar, leather, forest floor
